description: All interior trim carpentry and stair systems are installed, inspected, and approved by superintendent before painting begins.

categories.name: Crew & Schedule
items: Trim carpenter crew confirmed on-site and schedule locked with superintendent
Stair subcontractor or millwork fabricator confirmed and lead time verified against overall schedule
All drywall punch and texture complete, superintendent sign-off on file before trim crew mobilizes
Paint contractor notified of trim completion target date for back-scheduling prime coat
Sequencing confirmed: stair rough carriages inspected before treads and risers delivered

categories.name: Materials On-Site
items: All interior door units on-site, counted against door schedule, and verified correct swing, size, and hardware prep
Casing profile, base profile, and crown profile verified against client selections; samples approved by client in writing
Window and door casing stock acclimated on-site minimum 72 hours before installation
Stair treads (species, thickness, width) verified against approved selection and delivered undamaged; no cupping or splits exceeding 1/16 inch
Stair risers, skirt boards, newel posts, balusters, and handrail stock all on-site and counted against stair drawings
Craftsman or specialty millwork items (built-ins, wainscoting panels, coffered ceiling components) on-site and verified against shop drawings
Fastener and adhesive supplies on-site: finish nailers loaded, construction adhesive, wood filler, and sandpaper stock confirmed

categories.name: Pre-Install Verification
items: All rough openings for doors re-measured and confirmed within 1/4 inch of door unit dimensions before hanging
Wall plumb and flat tolerance verified at all trim locations — no deviation greater than 1/8 inch over 8 feet
Floor flatness verified at stair landing and base of stairs — no high spots exceeding 3/16 inch under a 10-foot straightedge
All blocking confirmed in walls at handrail mounting heights (34–38 inches AFF per IRC R311.7.8), stairwell, and built-in locations
HVAC registers, electrical boxes, and plumbing access panels located and marked so trim layout avoids conflicts
Subfloor at stair opening confirmed square and level; carriage attachment points inspected by superintendent

categories.name: Door Installation
items: All interior doors hung plumb and level; gap at head and strike jamb uniform, not to exceed 1/8 inch
Door bottoms set at correct clearance for flooring type per flooring schedule (tile vs. hardwood vs. carpet zones)
All doors operate without binding, racking, or spring-back; latch fully engages strike on first close
Door hardware prep (bore and latch holes) at correct backset per hardware selection; hardware not yet installed
Pocket door frames square and tracks aligned; doors slide full travel without deflection or floor drag
All bifold and barn door hardware tracks installed level and confirmed for door weight rating

categories.name: Trim & Millwork Installation
items: All window and door casing miters tight — no gap exceeding 1/32 inch at miter joint face
Base molding scribed or coped at inside corners; no butt joints visible at inside corners
Crown molding spring angle consistent throughout each room; ceiling and wall reveals uniform within 1/16 inch
Wainscoting or board-and-batten panels level, plumb, and evenly spaced per approved layout drawing
Coffered or beam ceiling components glued and nailed; no visible fastener heads without filler
All built-in shelving and cabinetry trim plumb, level, and scribed tight to wall with no gap exceeding 1/16 inch
All nail holes filled with non-shrink wood filler and sanded flush; no raw MDF edges visible on painted trim

categories.name: Stair Installation
items: Stair rise and run consistent throughout full run — no single riser varying more than 3/8 inch from any other riser per IRC R311.7.5
Tread nosing projection uniform at 3/4 inch to 1-1/4 inch per IRC R311.7.5.3
All treads and risers glued and fastened; no squeaks under 250-lb point load test by superintendent
Stair skirt boards scribed tight to treads and risers; no gap exceeding 1/16 inch at scribe
Newel posts anchored through tread or floor; no lateral movement under 200-lb lateral force at top of post
Handrail graspable profile confirmed (IRC R311.7.8.3 Type I or II); height 34–38 inches measured vertically from tread nosing
Baluster spacing confirmed at no greater than 4 inches clear per IRC R311.7.8.2; diagonal measurement at open triangle below rake rail also checked

categories.name: Quality & Punch
items: Superintendent full walk of all trim with raking light inspection; snipe list generated and assigned to crew before phase closes
All trim joints re-checked after 48 hours for any shrinkage gaps exceeding 1/16 inch; re-glued and re-filled as needed
Stair handrail continuous from top to bottom landing with no interruption; terminations return to wall per code
All doors re-swung after trim install to confirm no rub on casing; striker plates shimmed if needed
No damaged or out-of-profile molding pieces accepted; any paint-grade trim with surface defects visible from 36 inches replaced
Job site broom-clean and all trim offcuts removed before painter mobilizes

categories.name: Inspections & Sign-Off
items: Baldwin County building inspector final stair inspection scheduled and passed (guardrail, baluster spacing, handrail height, rise/run conformance)
Superintendent signs phase completion form confirming all items closed
Client walkthrough of trim and stairs completed; client signs Trim & Stairs Acceptance form or punch items documented and assigned
Any open punch items from client walkthrough logged in Cost Tracker with assigned trade, dollar amount, and deadline before phase is marked closed
